title: palicourea oreodoxa
intro: Palicourea oreodoxa, also known as the red ironbark, is a species of flowering plant in the family Rubiaceae. It is native to the tropical forests of Central and South America and has been used for centuries in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antiseptic properties. The health benefits of Palicourea oreodoxa include reducing fever, treating skin conditions such as acne and eczema, and aiding in the treatment of respiratory infections. In herbal preparations, Palicourea oreodoxa is often used as a decoction or infusion, and can be found in various forms including capsules, teas, and tinctures.
